message:
perf(stats): skip blocker queries when no potential ready candidates

br stats unconditionally ran two relatively expensive queries even on
projects where the answer was structurally guaranteed to be zero
ready issues:

  1. storage.has_external_dependencies(true) followed by
     resolve_external_dependency_statuses() and external_blockers()
     — touches every external workspace listed in config.

  2. storage.get_blocked_ids() — walks the full dependency edge cache
     to compute the canonical br-ready blocked set.

Both are pointless if there is not even one open, non-ephemeral,
non-wisp, non-pinned, non-template, not-deferred-into-the-future
issue, because the ready filter would be empty regardless of what the
blocker queries return.

Extract the candidate predicate into is_potential_ready_candidate(),
compute has_potential_ready_candidates once at the top of
execute_inner(), and gate both expensive queries on it. The blocker
query for the in-process blocked-by-blocks count is also short-
circuited when there are no active issues at all.

compute_summary() now receives the precomputed `now` and the boolean
flag so it does not recompute Utc::now() or repeat the candidate
walk. The ready filter reuses is_potential_ready_candidate() instead
of inlining the field-by-field check, removing a bug-prone duplicate.

Tests get a small compute_test_summary() helper so the existing
unit tests do not have to thread the new arguments by hand.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
